title: Converting a large number to scientific notation

steps.instruction: Place the decimal point after the first non-zero digit.
steps.working: The first non-zero digit is $7$, so the coefficient is $7.3$.

steps.instruction: Count how many places the decimal point moved.
steps.working: The decimal moved 4 places to the left: $73{,}000 \to 7.3$.

steps.instruction: Write the number in the form $a \times 10^n$.
steps.working: Since the decimal moved 4 places to the left, the power of 10 is positive: $n = 4$.

title: Converting from scientific notation to a standard number

steps.working: The power of 10 is $-2$, which means the decimal moves 2 places to the left.

steps.instruction: Move the decimal point in the coefficient.
steps.working: Start with $5.4$ and move the decimal 2 places to the left: $5.4 \to 0.54 \to 0.054$.

title: Multiplying numbers in scientific notation
problem: Calculate $(3 \times 10^4) \times (2.5 \times 10^3)$ and write the answer in scientific notation.

steps.instruction: Apply the product rule to the powers of 10.
Combine and check that the coefficient satisfies $1 \le a < 10$.
steps.working: $7.5 \times 10^7$. Since $1 \le 7.5 < 10$, this is already in correct scientific notation.
